titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD 1.4.Ebor.9

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=842 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 15:48:09.533502+00 | translation moa=843 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 15:48:09.544642+00

p.cl: Sciant Uniuersi Sancte Matris ęcclesię filii . ad quos littere Iste peruenerint 

anno ab incarnatione Domini . m . c . lx . vii . ad perpetue pacis reformationem . controuersia que diu agitata fuerat
inter ęcclesiam dunelmensem et ecclesiam croilandensem super uilla et ecclesia de ederham et omnibus pertinentiis suis  apud striuelin in presentia Willelmi illustris regis Scottorum . Ricardi episcopi Sancti Andręe . Engelranni Glascuensis episcopi . Gaufridi abbatis de dunfemelin . Willelmi abbatis de Mailros  Walthevi comitis . Nicholai cancellarii . Ricardi capellani . Dauid
olifard . et aliorum proborum hominum . tam clericorum quam laicorum . sopita est . et ab utraque . parte fide interposita  terminata hoc modo .

Scilicet quod abbas et capitulum croilandensis ecclesie renun
tiauerunt omni iuri quod habebant in uilla de hederham et ecclesia ipsius uille et pertinentiis suis . ita . quod eadem uilla et ęcclesia cum omnibus pertinentiis suis cesserunt ęcclesię dunelmensi absque
reclamatione in perpetuum . Prior uero et conuentus dunelmensis ecclesie nouem marcas argenti in denariis ęcclesię croilandensi in stanford apud ęcclesiam sancti leonardi sine
assignatione alicuius possessionis annuatim persoluent in perpetuum . videlicet sexaginta solidos in die sancte Agathe uirginie et martiris . post purificationem sanctę marię . et alios sexaginta
solidos in die sancti osuualdi regis et martiris post festum sancti petri ad uincula .

Testibus his . Gaufrido priore de eboraco . Iohanne monacho filio danielis . Magistro Ricardo de Coldingham .
Magistro Stephano . Roberto de edingun . Roberto de mainil . Geruasio medico . Hugone de lewes . Waltero de chettun . Rad’ de het . Willelmo de refectorio . Roberto
de Waltham . Gvidone dapifero . Adam de walesende . Roger de Lindeseie . Ambrosio de boldun . Gaufrido filio Achari . Roger camerario . Guidone maraschallo .
Bernardo bonefei . Guafrido de bastun .
Let all sons of holy Mother Church to whom these letters shall have come know:

in the year of the Lord's incarnation 1167, for the restoration of perpetual peace, the controversy which had for a long time been argued between the church of Durham and the church of Croyland over the toun and church of Edrom and all its pertinents, at Stirling, in the presence of William the illustrious king of the Scots, Richard bishop of St Andrews, Ingram bishop of Glasgow, Geoffrey abbot of Dunfermline, William abbot of Melrose, Waldeve the earl, Nicholas the chancellor, Richard the chaplain, David Oliphant, and other worthy men as well clerics as laity, was put to rest, and by an act of trust on both sides was concluded in this manner:

The abbot and chapter of Croyland church have renounced every right which they had in the toun of Edrom and the church of the same toun and its pertinents, so that they ceded the same toun and church with all its pertinents to the church of Durham without counter-claim in perpetuity. And the prior and monastic community of the churh cof Durham shall annually pay, in perpetuity, nine merks of silver in cash to the church of Croyland in Stanford at St Leonard's church without assignment of any possession, namely, sixty shillings on the day of Saint Agatha, Virgin and Martyr, after the Purification of Saint Mary, and the other sixty shillings on the day of Saint Oswald, King and Martyr, after the feast of Saint Peter in Chains.

With these witnesses: Geoffrey, prior of York; John the monk, son of Daniel; Master Richard of Coldingham; Master Stephen; Robert of Edington; Robert de Mesnil; Gervase the physician; Hugh of Lewes; Walter of Kettins; Ralph de Het; William of the refectory; Robert of Waltham; Guy the steward; Adam of Wallsend; Roger of Lindsay; Ambrose of Bolton; Geoffrey son of Achar; Roger the chamberlain; Guy the marischal; Bernard Bonfoi; Geoffrey de Baston.
